skill.name: github
skill.description: Use GitHub CLI commands for pull requests, issues, workflow runs, repositories, and targeted GitHub API calls.
skill.summary: Operate GitHub repositories through authenticated gh CLI workflows.
skill.author: ETeissonniere

security_audit.summary: The external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs use of git and gh CLI commands. The commands match the stated GitHub purpose and show no malicious intent, but several examples can mutate authenticated repositories. The weak cryptography findings are false positives from prose and placeholder text, not cryptographic code.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated External GitHub Commands
medium_findings.description: Static external command findings are true positives. The skill directs use of git and gh commands, including create, merge, approve, close, rerun, fork, clone, and API operations. This is legitimate for a GitHub skill, but it can change remote repository state when run with an authenticated account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains direct command examples for git and gh. The commands are consistent with the skill purpose, but authenticated gh operations can modify remote GitHub resources.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static weak cryptography detections are false positives. The cited lines contain front matter, the word descriptions, and placeholder body text such as Desc. No hashing, cipher, or cryptographic implementation appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible prose or command placeholders. There is no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms or any cryptographic code.

dangerous_patterns.title: State-Changing gh CLI Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill lists authenticated gh commands that can create pull requests, approve reviews, merge pull requests, close issues, rerun workflows, fork repositories, and call the GitHub API. These commands should require user confirmation before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and include remote write operations. The risk is operational misuse, not hidden malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Manage GitHub Workflows from Your Assistant
content.value_statement: GitHub work often requires switching between pull requests, issues, workflow runs, and repository pages. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear gh CLI workflows for common repository tasks.
actual_capabilities: Detects GitHub repositories by checking git remotes for github.com.
Verifies GitHub CLI authentication before repository operations.
Provides gh commands for pull request creation, review, diff, checkout, and merge workflows.
Provides gh issue commands for listing, viewing, creating, closing, and commenting.
Provides gh run commands for viewing, logging, watching, and rerunning workflow runs.
Uses gh api for GitHub operations not covered by standard CLI commands.
limitations: Requires the GitHub CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Does not validate repository permissions beyond gh auth status.
Can suggest commands that modify remote repositories when executed.
Requires real pull request IDs, issue IDs, repository names, titles, and body text.

use_cases.title: Prepare Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Create, inspect, review, and merge pull requests with consistent gh CLI commands.

use_cases.title: Triage Project Issues
use_cases.description: List assigned issues, inspect details, add comments, and close resolved work from the terminal.

use_cases.title: Investigate CI Failures
use_cases.description: Review workflow runs, fetch logs, watch active jobs, and rerun failed jobs after fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Check GitHub Access
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether this repository uses GitHub and confirm my gh authentication status before doing anything else.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current branch status, check for uncommitted changes, and help me create a pull request with a clear title and body.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Failed Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the latest failed GitHub Actions run, summarize the relevant logs, and recommend the next debugging step.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Review Comments with the API
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GitHub API through gh to inspect pull request review comments and summarize unresolved feedback.

output_examples.input: Check my GitHub status before opening a pull request.
output_examples.output: The repository remote points to GitHub. Authentication is active. The current branch should be checked for uncommitted changes before creating the pull request.

output_examples.input: Summarize the latest failed workflow run.
output_examples.output: The latest failed run is identified, the relevant log sections are reviewed, and the likely failing step is summarized with a recommended fix.

output_examples.input: Help me triage my assigned issues.
output_examples.output: Assigned issues are listed, each issue can be opened for context, and the assistant can prepare comments or close resolved issues after confirmation.

best_practices: Confirm the active repository and gh authentication before running commands.
Review commands that create, merge, close, fork, or rerun remote resources.
Use comments, logs, and diff views when more context is needed before action.
anti_patterns: Do not merge or approve pull requests without explicit user confirmation.
Do not rerun workflows repeatedly without checking the failure cause.
Do not place secrets or private tokens in issue comments, pull request bodies, or command arguments.

faq.question: Does this skill require GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on the gh CLI and a valid authenticated GitHub session.

faq.question: Can this skill create pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides the standard gh workflow for creating pull requests with titles and body text.

faq.question: Can it manage GitHub Actions runs?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers listing runs, viewing logs, watching active runs, and rerunning failed jobs.

faq.question: Does it work outside GitHub repositories?
faq.answer: No. It is intended for repositories whose git remote points to github.com.

faq.question: Can it call the GitHub API?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests gh api for operations that are not covered by higher-level gh commands.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because authenticated commands can change remote GitHub resources.
